verses.1: He who is often rebuked and stiffens his neck
verses.2: When the righteous thrive, the people rejoice;
verses.3: Whoever loves wisdom brings joy to his father;
verses.4: The king by justice makes the land stable,
verses.5: A man who flatters his neighbor
verses.6: An evil man is snared by his sin,
verses.7: The righteous care about justice for the poor.
verses.9: If a wise man goes to court with a foolish man,
verses.10: The bloodthirsty hate a man of integrity;
verses.11: A fool vents all of his anger,
verses.12: If a ruler listens to lies,
verses.13: The poor man and the oppressor have this in common:
verses.14: The king who fairly judges the poor,
verses.15: The rod of correction gives wisdom,
verses.16: When the wicked increase, sin increases;
verses.17: Correct your son, and he will give you peace;
verses.18: Where there is no revelation, the people cast off restraint;
verses.19: A servant can’t be corrected by words.
verses.20: Do you see a man who is hasty in his words?
verses.21: He who pampers his servant from youth
verses.22: An angry man stirs up strife,
verses.23: A man’s pride brings him low,
verses.24: Whoever is an accomplice of a thief is an enemy of his own soul.
verses.25: The fear of man proves to be a snare,
verses.27: A dishonest man detests the righteous,
